name: PerplexityBot Allowed
description: Verifies robots.txt does not block PerplexityBot. Blocking PerplexityBot is the most common reason for Perplexity invisibility.

criteria.pass: robots.txt has no Disallow for PerplexityBot, OR explicit Allow

sources: Sieve Rule #1487 (0.99): "Blocking PerplexityBot is the most common reason for Perplexity invisibility." Note: Cloudflare documented Perplexity using stealth crawlers to bypass blocks — blocking may not fully work regardless.

name: BingPreview Allowed
description: Verifies robots.txt does not block BingPreview. Perplexity uses Bing's index as its primary data source, so blocking BingPreview indirectly prevents Perplexity indexing.

criteria.pass: robots.txt has no Disallow for BingPreview

sources: Sieve Anti-pattern #803: "Perplexity uses Bing's index as primary data source. Blocking BingPreview prevents Bing from crawling, which prevents Perplexity from indexing."

name: GoogleBot Allowed
description: Verifies robots.txt does not block Googlebot on the audited page path. Blocking Googlebot removes the page from all Google surfaces including AI Overviews — it is all or nothing.

criteria.pass: robots.txt has no Disallow for Googlebot on audited page path

sources: Blocking Googlebot removes page from all Google surfaces including AI Overviews. Cannot block Googlebot for AI Overviews only — it's all or nothing.
notes: GATE CHECK — Googlebot block = no Google indexing = no AI Overviews

name: No nosnippet/max-snippet:0
description: Verifies the meta robots tag carries no nosnippet or max-snippet:0 directive. These directives prevent Google from generating any snippet, which excludes the page from AI Overviews selection.

criteria.pass: No nosnippet or max-snippet:0 in meta robots

fix_template: Remove nosnippet. Change to `max-snippet:-1` (unlimited) if snippet control needed.
sources: Sieve Rule #1441 (0.99): "Page with nosnippet or max-snippet:0 is excluded from AI Overviews selection." These directives prevent Google from generating any snippet, including AI Overviews.

name: Content in Raw HTML
description: Measures how much body text is extractable from the raw HTML response. JS-only content is invisible to AI crawlers — this is the #1 AI visibility blocker.
method_detail: WebFetch content volume vs Chrome rendered

criteria.pass: WebFetch extracts > 500 words of body text (SSR/static content)
criteria.fail: WebFetch extracts < 200 words (JS SPA — content invisible to AI crawlers)

fix_template: Implement SSR or SSG. Ensure all critical content is in initial HTML response.
sources: Sieve Rule #1481 (0.99): "Perplexity's crawler cannot access JS-rendered content." Vercel study: zero JS execution by GPTBot across 500M+ requests. This is the #1 AI visibility blocker.

name: Content Not Behind JS Accordions
description: Verifies FAQ/accordion content is present in the initial HTML rather than loaded via JavaScript on click. AI crawlers do not execute JS, click interactive elements, or expand accordions.

criteria.pass: FAQ/accordion content is in initial HTML (just visually collapsed via CSS)
criteria.warn: Accordion content appears to be in HTML but uses JS toggle (may be in DOM)
criteria.fail: Accordion content loaded via JavaScript on click (not in initial HTML)

fix_template: Use CSS-only accordions (`<details>/<summary>`) or render content in HTML with CSS visibility toggle
sources: Sieve Anti-pattern #804 (high): "Perplexity does not execute JavaScript, click interactive elements, expand accordions." Content must be in raw HTML to be extractable.

name: IndexNow or Ping Mechanism
description: Verifies an IndexNow key file or API integration exists. IndexNow notifies Bing (which powers Copilot), Yandex, and Seznam of content changes instantly; Google does not support it.

criteria.pass: IndexNow key file exists at `/[key].txt` or IndexNow API integration detected

fix_template: Implement IndexNow API integration. Generate key and host at `https://[domain]/[key].txt`
sources: IndexNow notifies search engines of content changes instantly. Supported by Bing (powers Copilot), Yandex, Seznam. Not supported by Google (uses its own crawl scheduling). Sieve Anti-pattern #763: "Waiting for standard crawl cycles instead of IndexNow" for time-sensitive content.

name: Page in XML Sitemap
description: Verifies the audited page URL appears in the XML sitemap. Sitemap inclusion helps crawlers discover and prioritize the page; lastmod is the only useful sitemap signal.
method_detail: Cross-ref page URL vs sitemap URLs

criteria.pass: Audited page URL appears in the sitemap

fix_template: Add page URL to sitemap.xml with accurate lastmod date
sources: Sitemap inclusion helps crawlers discover and prioritize pages. Missing = page may be discovered through links but not prioritized. lastmod in sitemap is the only useful signal (Google ignores priority/changefreq).

name: Bing Webmaster Verification
description: Verifies Bing site ownership markers (msvalidate.01 meta tag or BingSiteAuth.xml) are present. Bing Webmaster Tools provides the AI Performance Report with Copilot citation data.

criteria.pass: `<meta name="msvalidate.01" content="[key]">` present OR BingSiteAuth.xml exists

fix_template: Add `<meta name="msvalidate.01" content="[your key]">` from Bing Webmaster Tools
sources: Bing Webmaster Tools provides AI Performance Report (Feb 2026) showing Copilot citation data. Verification enables this diagnostic data. Also signals site ownership to Bing's index.

name: ClaudeBot/ChatGPT-User/Applebot Allowed
description: Verifies robots.txt does not block the ClaudeBot, ChatGPT-User, OAI-SearchBot, or Applebot crawlers. Each OpenAI/Anthropic/Apple bot is independently controllable, and ChatGPT accounts for 87.4% of all AI referral traffic.

criteria.pass: robots.txt has no Disallow for ClaudeBot, ChatGPT-User, OAI-SearchBot, Applebot
criteria.warn: Some but not all are allowed

fix_template: Ensure all AI crawlers are allowed. Note: blocking ChatGPT-User is ineffective since 2025.
sources: ChatGPT-User NO LONGER respects robots.txt (2025 policy change). Blocking GPTBot does NOT block OAI-SearchBot. Each OpenAI/Anthropic/Apple bot is independently controllable. ChatGPT = 87.4% of all AI referral traffic.

name: Content Not Behind Paywall/Login
description: Verifies primary content is accessible without login, paywall, or authentication. AI crawlers cannot authenticate, so login-gated content is completely invisible to GPTBot, PerplexityBot, and ClaudeBot.
method_detail: Check for login gates, paywall indicators, authentication requirements

criteria.pass: All primary content is accessible without login, paywall, or authentication
criteria.warn: Some content gated but primary answer content is accessible
criteria.fail: Core content requires login or payment to access

fix_template: Make core informational content freely accessible. Use paywall only for premium/extended content. If paywall required, implement `isAccessibleForFree` schema property and Google's paywall structured data.
sources: Sieve AP #810 (high): "Placing primary content behind authentication gates" — AI crawlers cannot authenticate. Login-gated content is completely invisible to GPTBot, PerplexityBot, ClaudeBot, and all AI crawlers. Even Google has limited ability to index paywalled content (requires structured paywall markup + First Click Free or flexible sampling).

name: No NOARCHIVE on AI-Targeted Pages
description: Verifies no noarchive directive is set via meta robots or the X-Robots-Tag header. NOARCHIVE prevents Bing caching, limiting Copilot to URL-only citation with no rich content extraction; it does not affect Google AI Overviews.
method_detail: Check meta robots and X-Robots-Tag for noarchive directive

criteria.pass: No `noarchive` directive in meta robots tag or HTTP X-Robots-Tag header
criteria.warn: `noarchive` present but page is not a primary content/service page (may be intentional on sensitive pages)
criteria.fail: `noarchive` present on a page intended for AI citation (service page, blog, product page)

fix_template: Remove `noarchive` from meta robots. Change `<meta name="robots" content="noarchive">` to `<meta name="robots" content="index, follow, max-snippet:-1">`. If noarchive is set via HTTP header (X-Robots-Tag: noarchive), remove it from server config.
sources: Sieve Rule #1424 (Bing, 0.98): "NOARCHIVE blocks Copilot citation." The noarchive directive prevents Bing from caching the page, which severely limits Copilot to URL-only citation — no rich content extraction, no answer blocks. Sieve AP#766 (high): "Applying NOARCHIVE Meta Tag to Content Intended for Copilot Citation." Note: noarchive does NOT affect Google AI Overviews (confirmed by Sieve Rule #1468, 0.93).

name: CCBot / LLM Training Crawler Access
description: Verifies robots.txt does not block CCBot (Common Crawl), the dataset underlying training of GPT-4, Claude, Llama, and most foundation LLMs. Blocking CCBot means the brand won't exist in future models' training data — training-time recognition, distinct from inference-time citation.
method_detail: Check robots.txt for CCBot Disallow or wildcard blocks

criteria.pass: robots.txt has no Disallow for CCBot (Common Crawl), OR explicitly allows CCBot
criteria.warn: robots.txt uses wildcard `Disallow: /` that covers CCBot but no explicit exception
criteria.fail: robots.txt explicitly blocks CCBot (`User-agent: CCBot / Disallow: /`) OR the site is excluded from Common Crawl index

fix_template: Add to robots.txt: `User-agent: CCBot\nAllow: /`. If wildcard `Disallow: /` is set, add explicit `Allow: /` for CCBot. Note: CCBot respects robots.txt, unlike some other AI crawlers.
sources: Common Crawl (CCBot) is the underlying dataset used to train GPT-4, Claude, Llama, and most foundation LLMs. Blocking CCBot means your brand won't exist in the training data of future AI models. Sieve Rule #2016 (amsive.com, 0.95): "AI Crawler Robots.txt Access Rule — If robots.txt blocks CCBot from accessing the site, the site is excluded from Common Crawl dataset." Unlike real-time AI crawlers (GPTBot, PerplexityBot), CCBot access affects how future AI models KNOW about your brand, not just how current ones retrieve it. Both matter: CCBot affects training-time brand recognition; GPTBot/PerplexityBot/ClaudeBot affect inference-time citation.
